title: U.S. Firms Introduce Joint Maritime Security Solution for Offshore Assets
summary: Janus Marine & Defense and Raven Defense Corporation have announced a partnership aimed at enhancing maritime security systems to protect offshore energy operations and shipping in the Gulf of America...
content:
Janus Marine & Defense and Raven Defense Corporation have announced a partnership aimed at enhancing maritime security systems to protect offshore energy operations and shipping in the Gulf of America. As industrial activities and commercial shipping traffic increase in this region, the two U.S. defense companies are responding to a growing demand for automated security solutions.

The collaboration combines Janus Marine’s expertise in autonomous surface and subsurface vessels with Raven Defense’s capabilities in satellite communication, unmanned aerial systems, intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR), as well as electronic warfare technologies. This partnership is designed to address the evolving security needs of offshore energy operators by integrating unmanned and networked capabilities that reduce the operational strain on U.S. security forces in high-risk maritime environments.

Janus Marine & Defense CEO Jack R. Dougherty emphasized the operational goal of establishing layered protection around maritime infrastructure. He noted that the Gulf of America is experiencing significant increases in naval and commercial shipping activities, necessitating advanced technologies to safeguard both assets and personnel. Dougherty mentioned that utilizing autonomous systems can help lower costs while maintaining vigilance and effectiveness, thus alleviating pressure on U.S. security forces.

Raven Defense Corporation CEO Chris Patscheck shared the vision of the collaboration, stating that the integration of persistent ISR, autonomous patrols, rapid interception, and remote operations center (ROC) support addresses the specific threats faced by offshore energy operators. The partnership aims to set new standards in the protection of energy infrastructure and shipping, utilizing unmanned aerial and aquatic platforms alongside advanced signal detection and cybersecurity measures.

The operational framework proposes a unified maritime security architecture where autonomous surface vessels and underwater systems perform monitoring tasks, while aerial drones and ISR networks enhance surveillance and threat detection capabilities. Remote operation centers will allow for coordinated responses without requiring constant on-site personnel.

Such autonomous systems are increasingly vital for monitoring extensive offshore infrastructure—like oil and gas platforms and shipping lanes—where traditional manned patrols are costly and impractical. These platforms can operate for extended durations, relaying sensor data and alerts to remote operators.

Moreover, satellite communications and electronic warfare capabilities are integral to this layered defense strategy, as they facilitate the detection of suspicious activities and potential interferences affecting maritime operations. The incorporation of ISR enables tracking of vessel movements and the identification of anomalies that might signify security threats.

This announcement comes at a time when commercial and naval activities in the Gulf region are expanding significantly, making the protection of energy infrastructure and maritime logistics networks vital to economic security. Continuous monitoring across vast operational areas is crucial, particularly under conditions where conventional patrol methods are insufficient.

Overall, the partnership between Janus Marine & Defense and Raven Defense Corporation reflects a forward-thinking approach to maritime security, leveraging advanced technologies to ensure the safety of critical infrastructure in a rapidly evolving operational landscape.
